title: Graph Attention Networks (GAT)
summary: Apply attention mechanism to graph edges. The bridge between GNNs and transformers. Self-attention on a fully-connected graph IS standard transformer attention. Cross-attention between graph K/V and query Q IS the knowledge-grounded attention pattern.
key_insights: Attention weight on edges: learned, not fixed by graph structure
Multi-head attention on graphs: same concept as transformer multi-head
Transformer = special case of GAT where the graph is fully connected
Cross-attention between graph K/V and query Q = knowledge-grounded attention

mathematical_core.update: h_v' = sigma(sum_{u in N(v)} alpha_vu * W * h_u)

notes: Theoretical bridge for knowledge graph + cross-attention application idea
